schema_notes: anchored to arxiv 2310.06825 (Mistral 7B) v1 PDF + 5 Wikipedia revisions PINNED via &oldid= URL parameter (immutable). The executor must (1) wget the paper, (2) extract its meta with poppler, (3) curl Wikipedia revids, (4) cross-check 5 concepts against the paper text, (5) author a single composite Inkscape SVG poster panel (≥1 rect + ≥8 text elements covering title/authors/page-count/5 concept boxes), (6) inkscape SVG→PDF export, (7) Evince screenshot. The Inkscape platform value is real composition (multiple visual elements with structure) — flat text dump fails the SVG and PDF checks. All values fetched 2026-05-02.

poster_svg_structure_requirements.rationale: The Inkscape platform value is real composition: structured layout with a background/border rect plus multiple text elements arranged into a poster panel — not a flat text dump.
expected_text_categories: authors (1 text, may be split across multiple)
5 concept boxes (≥5 text, can be more if split into name/summary/marker)

poster_pdf_required_strings.rationale: After inkscape exports the SVG to PDF, pdftotext should be able to extract every load-bearing string. Order does not matter; case-sensitive substring match unless noted.
poster_pdf_required_strings.concept_name_normalization: underscore <-> space accepted (e.g., 'Mistral_AI' or 'Mistral AI' both count)

accepted_encodings: Unicode U+2713 (✓) for verified, U+2717 (✗) for wiki_only
ASCII 'VERIFIED' for verified, 'WIKI ONLY' for wiki_only
marker_count_required.rationale: Either encoding is fine — what matters is the 3-vs-2 split matches the cross_check.csv.

multi_lang_availability_expected.rationale: Cross-Wikipedia language coverage via the MediaWiki langlinks API on the EN page of each anchored concept. Pulled 2026-05-02. Out of the 5 concepts, 4 are present on Japanese Wikipedia and all 5 are present on Chinese Wikipedia; only Mixture_of_experts is missing a ja edition. The asymmetry (zh more complete than ja) is a stable, easily verifiable cross-platform signal. Eval allows >=4 of 5 to match.
